title: Kubernetes Purpose, Architecture, Reconciliation, and Environment Evidence · Reconciliation Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in kubernetes purpose, architecture, reconciliation, and environment evidence, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Trace API server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager, kubelet, container runtime, network proxy, DNS, and add-on responsibilities for one workload transition.
Explain how API requests, admission, persisted desired state, watches, caches, and component status relate without treating any one cache as authority.
Trace desired state, observed state, controller decisions, idempotent actions, status updates, and eventual convergence through a changed failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Control Plane Node Components
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness. Competency probe: Trace API server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager, kubelet, container runtime, network proxy, DNS, and add-on responsibilities for one workload transition.

Inspect the K8s Control Plane Node Components trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Version Context Baseline
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-version-context-baseline-c8ln1q". Tailor its architecture-evidence.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Version Context Baseline.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Version Context Baseline into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Version Context Baseline, keep K8s Version Context Baseline connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-version-context-baseline-c8ln1q
  - competency: k8s-version-context-baseline
    artifact: architecture-evidence.yaml
    decision: version context baseline
    environment: kubeadm recovery cluster restored onto replacement nodes
    fault-injection: make an admission dependency unavailable during an upgrade
    retained-proof: authorization review token audience and audit correlation
    case-key: c8ln1q
    change: |
      request-path: kubectl -> api-server -> admission -> etcd
      controller-watch: desired-vs-observed
      node-path: scheduler -> kubelet -> runtime
      convergence: status-condition
    verify: kubectl version && kubectl cluster-info && kubectl get --raw /readyz?verbose
    failure: Reject client-version-only evidence, direct-etcd application access, or a claim that reconciliation guarantees application correctness.
    repair: Capture current context, server APIs, component authority, desired state, observed status, and the remaining distribution-specific boundary.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The K8s Version Context Baseline structural check is only one gate. cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Capture client, server, API discovery, context, cluster identity, node, runtime, add-on, feature-state, and support-window evidence before mutation.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Version Context Baseline, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Version Context Baseline precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Version Context Baseline, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness. Competency probe: Capture client, server, API discovery, context, cluster identity, node, runtime, add-on, feature-state, and support-window evidence before mutation.

For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Version Context Baseline, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: kubectl version alone proves the current context, server compatibility, and enabled APIs.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using capture and client plus specific case evidence.
